library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

identity.name: SDL_GetPrefPath
identity.titleArabic: جلب user- -app-specific المسار where الملفات can be written

summary.actualTranslationArabic: جلب user- -app-specific المسار where الملفات can be written
summary.actualMeaningArabic: جلب user- -app-specific المسار where الملفات can be written.
summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى جلب user- -app-specific المسار where الملفات can be written داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic:
القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه (char *) Returns a UTF-8 string of the user directory in
platform-dependent notation. NULL if there's a problem (creating
directory failed, etc.). This should be freed with SDL_free () when it is no longer needed. وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.
summary.sourceDescription: Get the user-and-app-specific path where files can be written.

parameters.name: org
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل org من النوع const char * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.

parameters.name: app
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل app من النوع const char * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.

remarks:
Get the "pref dir". This is meant to be where users can write
personal files (preferences and save games, etc) that are specific to
your application. This directory is unique per user, per
application.

This function will decide the appropriate location in the native
filesystem, create the directory if necessary, and return a string of
the absolute path to the directory in UTF-8 encoding.
On Windows, the string might look like:
On Linux, the string might look like:
On macOS, the string might look like:

You should assume the path returned by this function is the only safe
place to write files (and that SDL_GetBasePath (), while it might be
writable, or even the parent of the returned path, isn't where you
should be writing things).

Both the org and app strings may become part of a directory name, so
please follow these rules:

Try to use the same org string ( including case-sensitivity )
for all your applications that use this function.

Always use a unique app string for each one, and make sure it never
changes for an app once you've decided on it.

Unicode characters are legal, as long as they are UTF-8 encoded,
but...

...only use letters, numbers, and spaces. Avoid punctuation like
"Game Name 2: Bad Guy's Revenge!" ... "Game Name 2" is sufficient.

Due to historical mistakes, org is allowed to be NULL or
"". In such cases, SDL will omit the org subdirectory, including on
platforms where it shouldn't, and including on platforms where this
would make your app fail certification for an app store. New apps should
definitely specify a real string for org .

The returned path is guaranteed to end with a path separator ('\' on
Windows, '/' on most other platforms).
details.returns:
(char *) Returns a UTF-8 string of the user directory in
platform-dependent notation. NULL if there's a problem (creating
directory failed, etc.). This should be freed with SDL_free () when it is no longer needed.
details.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
